records.question: Which documents named Review on French files, dated on or before 1980-10-22 14:17:11, are linked to the Population Research project and have a CV type described as excellent with government expense details?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT documents.document_id FROM documents
JOIN projects ON documents.project_id = projects.project_id
JOIN ref_document_types ON documents.document_type_code = ref_document_types.document_type_code
JOIN documents_with_expenses ON documents.document_id = documents_with_expenses.document_id
WHERE ref_document_types.document_type_name = 'CV' AND ref_document_types.document_type_description = 'excellent' AND documents_with_expenses.document_details = 'government' AND projects.project_details = 'Population Research project' AND documents.document_date <= '1980-10-22 14:17:11' AND documents.document_name = 'Review on French files'
records.pred_sql:
select
  t1.document_id
from documents as t1
inner join projects as t2
  on t1.project_id = t2.project_id
inner join ref_document_types as t3
  on t1.document_type_code = t3.document_type_code
inner join documents_with_expenses as t4
  on t1.document_id = t4.document_id
inner join ref_budget_codes as t5
  on t4.budget_type_code = t5.budget_type_code
where
  t1.document_name like '%review on french files%'
  and t1.document_date <= '1980-10-22 14:17:11'
  and t2.project_details like '%population research%'
  and t3.document_type_description like '%excellent%'
  and t5.budget_type_description like '%government expense details%'
limit 5;
records.thought: The query requires filtering documents by name, date, project details, document type description, and budget type description. This necessitates joining the Documents table with Projects, Ref_Document_Types, Documents_with_Expenses, and Ref_Budget_Codes.

records.question: Which documents named Review on French files, dated on or after 1980-10-22 14:17:11, have a CV type described as excellent, are linked to the Population Research project, come from an Open Project statement, and include government expenses?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT documents.document_id FROM documents
JOIN projects ON documents.project_id = projects.project_id
JOIN ref_document_types ON documents.document_type_code = ref_document_types.document_type_code
JOIN statements ON documents.document_id = statements.statement_id
JOIN documents_with_expenses ON documents.document_id = documents_with_expenses.document_id
WHERE statements.statement_details = 'Open Project' AND documents.document_date >= '1980-10-22 14:17:11' AND documents.document_name = 'Review on French files' AND ref_document_types.document_type_description = 'excellent' AND ref_document_types.document_type_name = 'CV' AND documents_with_expenses.document_details = 'government' AND projects.project_details = 'Population Research project'
records.pred_sql:
select
  t1.document_id
from documents as t1
inner join ref_document_types as t2
  on t1.document_type_code = t2.document_type_code
inner join projects as t3
  on t1.project_id = t3.project_id
inner join statements as t4
  on t1.document_id = t4.statement_id
inner join documents_with_expenses as t5
  on t1.document_id = t5.document_id
inner join ref_budget_codes as t6
  on t5.budget_type_code = t6.budget_type_code
where
  t1.document_name like '%review on french files%'
  and t1.document_date >= '1980-10-22 14:17:11'
  and t2.document_type_description like '%excellent%'
  and t3.project_details like '%population research%'
  and t4.statement_details like '%open project%'
  and t6.budget_type_description like '%government expenses%'
limit 5;
records.thought: The query requires filtering documents by name, date, document type description, project details, statement details, and budget type description. This necessitates joining `Documents` with `Ref_Document_Types`, `Projects`, `Statements`, `Documents_with_Expenses`, and `Ref_Budget_Codes`.
